title: cerastium vulgatum subsp. caespitosum
intro: Cerastium vulgatum subsp. caespitosum, also known as the creeping chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of creeping chickweed include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide relief from digestive issues and stress. Creeping chickweed can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
